components.role: Unity-gain buffer — 100% negative feedback for lowest distortion and output impedance
Bias voltage divider upper — creates Vcc/2 virtual ground reference
Bias voltage divider lower — creates Vcc/2 virtual ground reference
Bias decoupling capacitor — stabilizes Vcc/2 reference, filters noise
Input coupling capacitor — blocks DC, sets low-frequency rolloff
Output coupling capacitor — blocks DC bias from output
Input resistor — provides DC path to bias point for non-inverting input
Power supply decoupling — filters supply noise

dsp_model.description: Unity gain pass-through with negligible coloration; only the coupling caps affect frequency response

behavioral_description: The most transparent buffer option — an op-amp wired for unity gain with 100% negative feedback. Provides extremely low distortion, very low output impedance (< 1Ω), and very high input impedance (set by the input resistor, typically 1MΩ). The sound is clinical and uncolored, passing the signal with virtually no tonal change. Best used where absolute transparency is needed, such as at the start of a complex signal chain or before a sensitive tone-shaping stage. The only frequency shaping comes from the coupling capacitors.
